title: Player notification opt-in on availability screen
description: Add a notification toggle/button to the player availability screen (/my-availability/{token}) that allows players to enable or disable push notifications. This registers the service worker, triggers the browser permission prompt, subscribes to push, and links the subscription to the player's token. The UI should clearly show the current notification state.
acceptance_criteria: Service worker is registered when the availability page loads
An 'Enable Notifications' button/toggle is visible on the availability screen
Clicking the button triggers the browser notification permission prompt
On permission grant: subscribes to push and sends subscription + player_token to server
Button state updates to reflect current status: enabled, disabled, or blocked (permission denied)
If notifications are already enabled, button shows 'Disable Notifications' and allows unsubscribing
If browser permission was previously denied, button shows explanatory text with instructions to unblock in browser settings
Works on Chrome (desktop + Android), Firefox, Safari (iOS 16.4+ and macOS), Edge
On iOS Safari: detects if app is not installed as PWA and prompts to Add to Home Screen first (push requires PWA on iOS)
Player token is passed to the subscription endpoint so the subscription is linked to the player
Visual feedback during subscription process (loading state)
Notification state persists across page reloads

technical_notes.ui_placement: Add notification toggle near the top of the availability page, below the player name header
technical_notes.service_worker_registration: Add <script> to layout.html that registers /service-worker.js on page load. This is currently missing.
ios_considerations: Push notifications on iOS require the app to be installed as a PWA (Add to Home Screen)
Detect standalone display mode via window.matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)')
If not standalone on iOS, show 'Add to Home Screen' instructions before enabling notifications
iOS Safari 16.4+ supports Web Push but only in standalone/PWA mode
existing_infrastructure: static/push.js already has subscribeToPushNotifications(), unsubscribeFromPushNotifications(), checkPushSubscription()
These functions need to be updated to pass player_token
Service worker at static/service-worker.js already handles push events
technical_notes.player_token_availability: The player token is available in the URL path (/my-availability/{token}) and should be passed to the template as a data attribute or JS variable

testing_requirements: Test enable flow on Chrome desktop
Test enable flow on Android Chrome
Test enable flow on iOS Safari (PWA mode)
Test permission-denied state shows correct guidance
Test that subscription is correctly linked to the player token
Test page reload preserves notification state

context.user_story: As a player, I want to turn on notifications from my availability page so I get alerts when I'm selected for a match or need to update my availability
context.existing_code: static/push.js has the subscription logic, templates/players/availability.html is the target page
